rulings.Case Title: MARK A. BROWN VS. CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Further Enforce Writ On Remand From Court Of Appeal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 19, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER MARK BROWN's Motion To Further Enforce Writ On Remand From Court Of Appeal Petitioner Mark Brown's motion to further enforce writ of mandate on remand from the Court of Appeal is denied. The only issues raised by Mr. Brown's motion are the appropriate rate for postjudgment interest and whether there is delayed accrual of postjudgment interest. The Court of Appeal addressed both issues and this court is not at liberty to reach a different decision. Even if this court had the authority to reach a different decision than the Court of Appeal, this court declines to do so out of respect for the considered decision of the Court of Appeal. The Court of Appeal stated: "Pursuant to subdivisions (a) and (c) of section 965.5 of the Government Code, interest on a 'judgment for the payment of money against the state or a state agency' commences to accrue '180 days from the date of the final judgment or settlement.' The rate is the constitutional rate of 7 percent." (Brown v. California Unemployment Ins. Appeals Bd. (2018) 20 Cal.App.5th 1107, 1120). There is nothing equivocal or uncertain about the quoted language. None of the exceptions to the law of the case doctrine applies. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for EDD is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: PAT CASTANEDA ET AL VS. PHOENIX HOLDINGS GROUP, INC. A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Deem Truth Of Fats And Genuiness Of Documents Admitted, Monetary Sanctions; Compel Responses To And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Thursday, July 18, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANTS AGNES FLORENDO, ALICIA ATIENZA, PHOENIX HOLDINGS GROUP, INC., STATEWIDE REALTY & FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC.'S Motion To Deem Truth Of Fats And Genuiness Of Documents Admitted, Monetary Sanctions; Compel Responses To And Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED TO DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO DEEM TRUTH OF FACTS AND GENUINENESS OF DOCUMENTS AND COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION AS FOLLOWS: (1) PLAINTIFFS MUST WITHIN FIVE (5) DAYS FROM THE ORDER SERVE WITHOUT OBJECTION FULL ANC COMPLETE CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO EACH REQUEST FOR ADMISSION OF FACTS AND GENUINESS OF DOCUMENTS. IF PLAINTIFF FAILS TO COMPLY EACH FACT WILL BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND THE GENUINESS OF DOCUMENTS ESTABLISHED. PLAINTIFF WILL BE PRECLUDED FROM CHALLENGING ANY SUCH FACT OR THE GENUINES OF ANY SUCH DOCUMENT AT THE TRIAL OF THIS MATTER. (2) PLAINTIFF WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER PROVIDE, WITHOUT OBJECTION, ALL DOCUMENTS CALLED FOR IN DEFENDANTS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. (3) MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,000.00 WILL BE ASSESSED AGAINST PLAINTIFFS AND THEIR COUNSEL, PAYMENT OF WHICH MUST BE MADE TO DEFENDANTS AND THEIR COUNSEL WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON JULY 19, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD FIERO VS. GOLDEN GATE BRIDGE, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 19, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE BRIDGE, HIGHWAY, AND TRANSPORTATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Defendant Golden Gate Bridge, Highway and Transportation District's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Plaintiff Richard Fiero has submitted sufficient evidence to satisfy his minimal burden for a prima facie case for discrimination on the ground of mental disability. The District's evidence of the 2016 incident and the ensuing investigation shifted the burden to Mr. Fiero to produce substantial evidence creating a triable issue that the District's proffered legitimate non-discriminatory reasons of poor performance and dishonesty for the termination of Mr. Fiero's employment were pretextual. Construing the evidence in a light most favorable to Mr. Fiero and resolving all doubts in favor of Mr. Fiero, the evidence submitted on this motion shows that there is a triable issue of pretext based on 1) Mr. Swindler's question about Mr. Fiero's compliance with the condition imposed for the continuing validity of his license to submit to annual psychiatric examinations and 2) Mr. Swindler's awareness of the recent investigation into Mr. Fiero's stability in relation to workplace safety and guns. Based on reasonable inferences drawn from the foregoing evidence, a reasonable trier of fact could find that Mr. Fiero's depression was a substantial motivating reason for Mr. Swindler's decision to terminate Mr. Fiero's employment. (See Wallace v. County of Stanislaus (2016) 245 Cal.App.4th 109, 129 (disability animus not required)). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Fiero is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: LILLIAN FORMAN ET AL VS. SUTTER HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 19, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANTS SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS DBA SUTTER SANTA ROSA and CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Sutter Bay Hospitals' demurrer to the third through sixth causes of action in plaintiffs' second amended complaint is overruled in its entirety. Plaintiffs sufficiently allege claims for negligent and intentional misrepresentation. Paragraph 18 alleges that Dr. Weinert was the ostensible agent of the Hospital. Paragraphs 74, 106, and 112 allege that Dr. Weinert represented that he knew the CPMC doctors and they were excellent even though this statement was false. These allegations are sufficiently particular to support the misrepresentation claims. (Mejia v. Community Hospital of San Bernardino (2002) 99 Cal.App.4th 1448, 1453 ("the overwhelming majority of jurisdictions employed ostensible or apparent agency to impose liability on hospitals for the negligence of independent contractor physicians.")). The Hospital's reply argument that the ostensible agency theory fails because Julie Forman executed a Conditions of Admission form is not amenable to resolution on this demurrer. The Hospital improperly relies on extrinsic evidence to support the argument. The lack of informed consent claim is adequately pled based on the allegation that Dr. Shu failed to obtain Ms. Forman's informed consent and Dr. Shu was the ostensible agent of the Hospital. While the Hospital is correct that the informed consent claim is subsumed by the negligence claim, there is no prejudice to the Hospital by the separate pleading of an informed consent claim and some clarity is achieved by plaintiffs alleging different species of negligence in separate claims. The negligent infliction claim is also adequately stated. The allegations that plaintiffs Chance Forman and Krista Thrasher observed Dr. Shu break Lillian's bones and violently yanked her are sufficient to show contemporaneous awareness of the injury-producing event. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: LILLIAN FORMAN ET AL VS. SUTTER HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 19, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT RACHEL SHU's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Rachel Shu's motion to strike the entirety of the second amended complaint is granted with twenty days leave to amend. The second amended complaint is fairly characterized as a gratuitous hit piece on Dr. Shu that makes reference to and quotes an array of negative opinions about Dr. Shu that are neither necessary nor proper for plaintiffs to adequately state their claims. The third amended complaint should be written without inflammatory language and should not include any quotes from social media or irrelevant ad hominem terms such as "monster" and "serious personality disorder." With regard to the allegations that defendants knew or should have known that Dr. Shu was not competent to perform the delivery of Lillian, the third amended complaint should focus only on ultimate facts, keeping in mind that California law does not require a pleading to allege evidentiary facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Dr. Shu is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: GWENDOLYN COLEMAN VS. ARCHIE P. HALL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Appearance Of Defendant Archie P. Hall At Deposition And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Thursday, July 18, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF GWENDOLYN COLEMAN'S Motion To Compel Appearance Of Defendant Archie P. Hall At Deposition And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF GWENDOLYN COLEMANS'S, MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT ARCHIE P. HALL IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS A RIGHT TO TAKE THE DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT ABSENT A SHOWING OF EXTRAORDINARY CIRCUMSTANCES. DEFENDANT HAS INDICATED A WILLINGNESS TO SUBMIT TO A DEPOSITION IN THE LATER PART OF AUGUST AND THE COURT WILL ORDER THAT DEFENDANT WILL APPEAR FOR HIS DEPOSITION NO LATER THAN AUGUST 30, 2018 AND MAY BE ACCOMPANIED BY HIS DOCTOR IF HE WISHES. THE COURT IS AWARE THAT PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT BY AUGUST 2, 2018 AND IF PLAINTIFF BELIEVES THAT HE NEED TO COMPLETE THE DEPOSITION BEFORE FILING HE CAN SEEK RELIEF AS APPROPRIATE. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT IT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON JULY 19, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN FITCH VS. DEPARTMENT OF ELECTIONS
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 19, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT DEPARTMENT OF ELECTIONS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Department of Elections for the City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the entirety of the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff John Fitch is sustained without leave to amend. With the election of the new mayor, all of Mr. Fitch's claims are now moot. Even if those claims were not moot, they lack merit as a matter of law because Mr. Fitch is not entitled to be mayor. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the Department of Elections is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
